title: chrysanthemum capusii
intro: Chrysanthemum capusii, also known as the yellow chrysanthemum or yomogi, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves. Chrysanthemum capusii is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant's flowers and leaves are often used in herbal preparations, and it can be consumed as a tea, taken as a supplement, or applied topically to the skin as a salve.
